vocab.meaning: một khoảng thời gian khó khăn để học kỹ năng mới
vocab.example: I found the advanced statistics module quite daunting as it had a incredibly steep learning curve.

vocab.word: burn the candle at both ends
vocab.meaning: làm việc quá sức, thức khuya dậy sớm
vocab.example: During finals week, I often have to burn the candle at both ends to keep up with the heavy workload.

vocab.word: bite off more than one can chew
vocab.ipa: /baɪt ɒf mɔːr ðæn wʌn kæn tʃuː/
vocab.meaning: ôm đồm quá nhiều thứ, quá sức mình
vocab.example: I think I bit off more than I could chew by enrolling in five elective courses this semester.
To be honest, some of the theoretical lectures are as dry as dust and fail to capture my interest.

vocab.meaning: ngập đầu trong công việc/bài tập
vocab.example: The only thing I dislike is being constantly snowed under with assignments and lab reports.
I'm not a fan of having to hit the books every single weekend instead of socializing with friends.

vocab.meaning: vượt qua (kỳ thi) với điểm số rất cao
vocab.example: While I struggle with the pressure, the feeling of passing a difficult exam with flying colors is quite rewarding.

vocab.word: put something on the back burner
vocab.meaning: tạm gác lại một việc gì đó (để ưu tiên việc khác)
vocab.example: Because of my thesis, I’ve had to put my personal hobbies on the back burner, which is quite frustrating.

vocab.word: it's not my cup of tea
vocab.meaning: không phải sở thích/thứ mình thích
vocab.example: Rote learning and memorizing long lists of dates is really not my cup of tea.

vocab.meaning: tháp ngà (ám chỉ môi trường học thuật xa rời thực tế)
vocab.example: Sometimes I feel like my course is stuck in an ivory tower, focusing too much on theory rather than practical application.
